AC_Multiple_h1_Headings_Usability_01 (AAA)


Description

User Story
As a screen reader user,
I want the page heading structure to follow Welcome to GOV.UK  conventions,
so that I can understand and navigate the page structure easily using heading levels.

Background 
On the Product Favourites page within the Create a UK catch certificate journey, there are two H1 headings present on the page. This does not align with Welcome to GOV.UK  Design System conventions and may cause confusion for users who rely on semantic headings to navigate content.

URL

https://fes-ext.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/create-catch-certificate/GBR-2026-CC-0F0C2569E/manage-favourites

Journey
Journey 1, Step 12

Issue Description
The page contains two <h1> elements:

“Product Favourites”

“Your product favourites”

Having multiple H1 headings on a single page may affect screen reader users who rely on heading levels to understand page structure and purpose. Users would typically expect a single H1 introducing the page, with subsequent headings marked up as H2s or lower.

Screen reader feedback (JAWS):

“Viewing the page with JAWS may confuse some users, as I found duplicated headings at a level 1. Although this is not a significant problem for me as an experienced user, a consistent heading structure will follow the conventions of the Welcome to GOV.UK  Design System and be easier to identify for users who rely on heading levels.”

Proposed Solution
Mark “Your product favourites” as an <h2> heading, as it is a sub-heading of “Product Favourites”. This will ensure a clear, logical heading structure and improve navigability for screen reader users.

Acceptance Criteria
The page contains only one H1 heading

“Product Favourites” remains marked up as the H1

“Your product favourites” is marked up as an H2

The heading structure follows Welcome to GOV.UK  Design System guidance
